source: MSC Cruises official site, cruise reviewer consensus, and onboard photography — no official PDF primary source available.
disclaimer: MSC does not publicly distribute PDF menus like some cruise lines. Menu items listed here reflect reported offerings from multiple cruise reviewers with 3+ source agreement. Menus change frequently and vary by ship, itinerary, and season.

notes: Available on most newer MSC ships. Pricing and exact menu items vary by ship.
Wine pairing options available for additional charge.
Reservations recommended — book through MSC for Me app.
All items priced individually (a la carte). Available on most MSC ships.
Counter seating and table options available.
Set multi-course meal prepared tableside by teppanyaki chef.
All entrees served with fried rice and stir-fried vegetables.
Limited seating — advance reservation required.

hola-tacos-cantina.pricing: Two pricing models on the same menu. All-you-can-eat (AYCE) roughly $24.99 per person in 2026 (was $20 earlier in 2025). À la carte: street tacos ~$2.39 each, burritos ~$10.79, enchiladas ~$11.99, sides and quesadillas $2.99-$5.99. Drinks separate: bottled beer ~$9, Chelada/Michelada $9.99-$15.99, margarita variations and tequila/mezcal flights priced individually.
notes: On MSC Seashore (Deck 8 Atrium), MSC Seascape, MSC Bellissima, MSC Meraviglia, MSC Virtuosa, MSC World Europa, and MSC World America (Deck 7 Terraces District over the Galleria). Reported expansion to MSC Seaside on later sailings.
Reservations recommended via the MSC for Me app — prime dinner slots fill on sea days.

Reviewers consistently recommend ordering à la carte unless you can put away four-plus tacos, and recommend skipping the multi-meal MSC dining package here in favour of Butcher's Cut or Kaito Teppanyaki where the per-meal value is stronger.
Tableside guacamole and the Carne Asada and Al Pastor tacos are the recurring picks; burritos and nachos under load are the most-flagged misses.
Named after MSC's private island Ocean Cay in the Bahamas.
Available on Meraviglia, Seashore, Seascape, Seaside, and Seaview.
Seafood-focused with Mediterranean and Caribbean influences.

eataly.pricing: A la carte pricing (varies by station).
notes: First Eataly at sea — exclusively on MSC World America.
Multiple dining stations in an Italian marketplace format.
Some items covered by dining packages; premium items priced separately.

asian-market-kitchen.pricing: Specialty cover charge per concept (~$49 sushi / ~$55 teppanyaki / ~$55 Pan-Asian fleet-wide reference). Bundle savings via MSC dining packages (2 meals ~$99, 3 meals ~$139). MSC Seaside and MSC Seaview ONLY — NOT the same as MSC's Kaito brand on other ships.
notes: Chef-collaboration with Roy Yamaguchi specific to the original Seaside class. Deck 16 Miami Beach, forward of the Bridge of Sighs.
Three connected rooms share one kitchen pass: Pan-Asian dining (32 seats), sushi counter (42 seats), teppanyaki (32 seats).
Teppanyaki entrees include fried rice and stir-fried vegetables prepared tableside.
Allergy and dietary requests handled at booking through the host — the kitchen pre-plates around shellfish, gluten, and dairy.
Reservations required via MSC for Me app; prime evening slots fill first on 3- and 4-night sailings.

yacht-club-restaurant.pricing: Complimentary for MSC Yacht Club suite guests — included with YC suite booking. Table wines and most drinks included; premium reserve bottles may carry an upcharge through the sommelier.
notes: Private MSC Yacht Club dining room — suite guests only. Open seating with no fixed times and no assigned tables; host seats by name within minutes.
On MSC Seaside: Deck 18, on the balcony directly above the Top Sail Lounge.
Menu changes daily on a roughly 7-night rotation; on a 4-night sailing you'll see about half of it.
Sea-day seafood lunch is widely cited as the sleeper highlight of the YC food program.
Allergy and dietary requests handled through the butler or concierge — the kitchen pre-plates the next day's menu so the chef can route around gluten, shellfish, dairy, or nut allergies.
Sommelier works the floor as a separate role; ask for off-list reserve bottles transparently.
